title: dumb-agent forbidden_paths symmetry — every SSOT data file pairs with its schema

evidence.ref: Audit pre-D031: agent-roles.riido.json had purpose-banlist.riido.json + purpose-banlist.schema.json (the only data+schema pair correctly listed). The other three SSOTs (ssot-dependency-map, concept-map, agent-roles itself) only listed data files; schemas were missing from forbidden_paths
018-fixture-purpose-banlist — set the (data, schema) pair convention by adding both purpose-banlist files; D031 brings the other three SSOTs into symmetry
009-agentguard-validate-and-roles-schema — introduced agent-roles.schema.json; D031 closes the gap that the schema itself was not forbidden

guards.ref: agent-roles.riido.json — dumb-agent forbidden_paths extended with agent-roles.schema.json, concept-map/schema.riido.json, ssot-dependency-map.schema.json
internal/agentguard/agentguard_test.go TestD031ForbidsSSOTSchemasSymmetrically — iterates over all four (data, schema) pairs and asserts both halves trigger Check kind=forbidden
concept-map C-016 — codifies the symmetric pairing convention so future SSOT additions register both files

examples: agent-roles.schema.json: dumb-agent attempting to weaken role validation by editing the schema is now rejected with `FORBIDDEN: matches forbidden_paths pattern "agent-roles.schema.json"`.
concept-map/schema.riido.json: similarly rejected; previously implicitly off-limits via 'not in allowed_paths' but the explicit listing matches the project's discoverable-defense convention.
Future SSOT additions must register both halves; the convention is documented in AGENT_CONTRACT.md and codified in C-016.
counterexamples: decisions/schema.riido.json is covered by the broader `decisions/**` glob; not double-listed.
ir/schema/ir.schema.json is covered by `ir/schema/**`; not double-listed.
ir/domain/ data files are NOT in forbidden_paths because dumb-agent's IR work surface is ir/candidates/ (separate sandbox).
